title: Schema Translation Catalog
description: Wave 133d / Plan-2 P1#7 (Wave 136a per-CURIE overlay merge): Per-family schema-to-English translation catalog. Lives at schemas/training/schema_translation_catalog.<family>.yaml. Loaded by Trainforge.generators.schema_translation_generator._load_form_data, dispatched on PropertyManifest.family. Wave 136a replaces Wave 133d's whole-family-swap behavior with a per-CURIE overlay merge: the in-Python _RDF_SHACL_FALLBACK_FORM_DATA dict is the base, and the YAML overlay merges on top per-CURIE so a partial YAML cannot erase complete in-Python entries. New course families ship their YAML against this schema.

family.description: Course-family identifier matching the corresponding property_manifest.<family>.yaml's family field. Optional but recommended for cross-reference.

description.description: Optional one-paragraph rationale: which surface forms this catalog covers and why.

forms.description: Map keyed by primary CURIE (e.g. 'sh:datatype'). Each entry seeds the six template families authored by the generator (definition / usage / comparison / reasoning / pitfall / combination). CURIEs absent from this map are skipped with a warning at synthesis time.

short_name.description: Human-readable short name for the construct (e.g. 'datatype' for sh:datatype). Used in decision-capture rationale interpolation.

anchored_status.description: Wave 135a discriminator. 'complete' entries carry real anchored content fit for embedding in training pairs. 'degraded_placeholder' entries satisfy the structural contract via stub strings but fall back to token-stuffing during force-injection (Wave 135b). Wave 136a's overlay merge uses this field to surface complete->degraded regressions in YAML overlays via logger.warning.

definitions.description: Hand-curated definition strings rendering the CURIE in different cognitive angles (formal-spec / pedagogical / context-anchored / etc.). Each becomes one schema_translation.definition pair via a rotating prompt frame.

usage_examples.description: List of (prompt, completion) pairs showing concrete TTL usage. Authored fully — no prompt frame applied. Each becomes one schema_translation.usage pair.

comparison_targets.description: List of (other_curie, contrast_explanation) pairs. The generator wraps with a comparison prompt frame; the completion must contain BOTH the primary CURIE and the secondary CURIE literally for preserve_tokens recognition.

reasoning_scenarios.description: List of (scenario_prompt, completion_with_curie) pairs probing applied reasoning. Authored fully.

pitfalls.description: List of (scenario_prompt, completion_with_curie) pairs documenting error modes + corrections. Authored fully.

combinations.description: List of (other_curie, composition_explanation) pairs. The generator wraps with a combination prompt frame; the completion must contain BOTH the primary CURIE and the secondary CURIE literally.

provenance.description: Wave 137c: operator/Codex/Qwen attribution + ToS audit trail. Required for anchored_status='complete' entries (Plan A's validator enforces); optional for 'degraded_placeholder' entries (no content yet to attribute). Not required at the per-CURIE schema level so degraded entries can omit cleanly; Plan A's MISSING_PROVENANCE rule reads getattr(entry, 'provenance', None) and rejects complete entries without one.

generated_by.description: Tool or operator that produced the content (e.g. 'draft_form_data_entry v1.0', 'operator').

reviewed_by.description: Operator handle who reviewed the entry (e.g. '@mdmurphy822'), 'AUTOMATED', or 'PENDING_REVIEW'. Plan A rejects entries with reviewed_by='PENDING_REVIEW' or empty.

prompt_version.description: Prompt-template version used for Qwen-drafted content (e.g. 'wave-136c-v1.0'). Use 'n/a-pre-wave-136c' for operator-hand-curated entries.
